title: Primary elections in Ohio, Indiana offer latest temperature check on Trump popularity
description: The president seeks to oust Indiana Republicans who blocked a redistricting push in that state, while growing dissatisfaction with Trump's agenda gives Democrats an opening in Ohio.

title: Ohio holding primary elections for several key races
description: Ohio is holding primary elections for key races, including governor, U.S. Senate and the House. Many voters say they are concerned about the rising cost of living.

title: Tuesday's election in Indiana puts Trump's influence to the test
description: Tuesday will be a test of President Trump's political power as voters in Indiana decide whether to back state senate incumbents Trump wants out or the challengers he has endorsed.

title: Voters head to the polls in Indiana and Ohio. What's in the balance?
description: What's at stake for President Trump's political influence in Tuesday's primaries in Indiana and Ohio? NPR's Leila Fadel asks Jessica Taylor of the Cook Political Report.

title: Alabama holding special session to redraw congressional maps
description: The Alabama Legislature is holding a special session this week to change the state's congressional map. The move comes after the U.S. Supreme Court weakened a central part of the Voting Rights Act.

title: Supreme Court clears path for Louisiana to redraw map in redistricting fight
description: The justices’ action follows last week’s opinion weakening a central element of the Voting Rights Act.

title: Second judge maintains DOJ can’t search data seized from Post reporter
description: The Justice Department had seized a phone and computers belonging to The Washington Post’s Hannah Natanson as part of a leak investigation.

title: Gunfire between Secret Service and armed person briefly locks down White House
description: The altercation near the Washington Monument occurred shortly after Vice President J.D. Vance passed by in a motorcade, a U.S. Secret Service official said.

title: Pirro will not appeal judge’s quashing of Fed subpoenas
description: U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro’s office has dropped plans to appeal a ruling by a federal judge that quashed two subpoenas to the Federal Reserve and its chairman.

title: White House lawyers prep staff for dealing with a Democratic Congress
description: The private briefings on best practices for handling congressional oversight come as polls indicate Democrats have a strong edge ahead of the November midterms.

title: Supreme Court restores access to abortion pill by mail for now
description: The order reinstates mail access to the drug for one week while the justices consider emergency applications by drugmakers.

title: Rudy Giuliani in critical condition, recovering from pneumonia, spokesman says
description: The former New York mayor, 81, initially was placed on a ventilator but now is breathing on his own, his spokesman said.

title: The State Department has had a surge in new retirements
description: Usually, 2/3 of U.S. ambassadors come from the career foreign service. But the Trump administration has named mostly political ambassadors, leaving senior career diplomats with few prospects.

title: The U.S. fights to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as UAE says it's attacked by Iran
description: The U.S. military said it battled Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while the United Arab Emirates said Iran fired missiles and drones its way.

title: The many ways Trump wants to change D.C., from buildings to statues to parks
description: Many of the changes Trump wants to make to the White House and the city are facing legal challenges. Some are reversible, while others could reshape the nation's capital for generations to come.

title: Iran threatens retaliation after Trump says U.S. will guide ships in Strait of Hormuz
description: The president left unclear what measures the U.S. would take and whether any forces would be at risk. Iran said it would strike any foreign force approaching the strait.

title: How the SCOTUS voting rights ruling could affect Congress, according to Eric Holder
description: Eric Holder, chairman of the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, talks with NPR's Michel Martin about a Supreme Court ruling that could pave the way for a drop in Black representation in Congress.

title: In many states, election-denying candidates are running to control voting
description: In 23 states, including five presidential swing states, candidates who have denied election results are running for offices that have a direct role in certifying future elections, a new report finds.

title: Trump hosts PGA Tour at Doral — and he’s the big winner
description: For a decade, golf’s biggest circuit shunned the president. In his second term, it has welcomed him back.

title: Pirro says ballistic evidence shows correspondents’ dinner suspect shot officer
description: A buckshot pellet from the suspect’s shotgun “was intertwined with the fiber of the vest” worn by a Secret Service officer, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said.
